lever_summary: F64 OneHot now returns the mostly-off tensor as LiteralBuffer::from_repeated_with_patches(vec![off], total, patches), using the same patch index calculation as the dense fill+scatter path. This avoids filling and storing the 1,048,576-element F64 output on construction while preserving materialized public literal order and bits.

alien_graveyard.primitive: sparse/lazy materialized tensor representation for a structurally sparse dense logical output
alien_graveyard.mapping: OneHot is a repeated off-value tensor with one sparse on-value patch per valid input index. The lever changes representation only, not the logical dense tensor.

baseline.command: RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings' rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline one_hot_2048x512_f64 -- --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3 --sample-size 10

candidate.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 RCH_WORKER=vmi1153651 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings' rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline one_hot_2048x512_f64 -- --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3 --sample-size 10

change.lever: Route DType::F64 eval_one_hot through one_hot_repeated_patch_buffer and TensorValue::new_with_literal_buffer. Other dtypes keep their existing dense or literal fill+scatter paths.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: patch generation enumerates input flat indices in the same order as one_hot_scatter and uses the same input-stride to output-stride mapping; LiteralBuffer materialization is row-major over the same total length.
isomorphism.tie_breaking: not applicable for one_hot; if duplicate patches were ever produced, LiteralBuffer materializes patches in vector order with last write winning, matching dense assignment order.
isomorphism.floating_point: no arithmetic added; on/off are exactly literal_for(on_value/off_value), preserving F64 bits including -0.0 and infinity in the golden.
isomorphism.errors: num_classes parse, dtype parse, axis validation, element-count overflow, input integer validation, and TensorValue element-count checks are unchanged.
isomorphism.storage_contract: F64 OneHot output is no longer as_f64_slice-dense; public materialized literals, dtype, shape, serde, iteration, and indexing remain equivalent.

validation.name: focused_one_hot_repeated_patches
validation.command: RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings' rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib one_hot_f64_repeated_patches_materialize_like_dense_scatter -- --nocapture
validation.result: passed on vmi1293453: 1 passed; final rerun after clippy-gate hygiene also passed on vmi1293453
